Politics.headline: Laura Fernandez sworn in as Costa Rica's new president
Politics.detail: Fernandez enters office with her right-wing party holding an absolute majority in the legislature.

Politics.headline: South Africa's top court revives impeachment inquiry against president
Politics.detail: The ruling allows for a renewed investigation into President Cyril Ramaphosa.

Politics.headline: GOP speaker claps back after Booker stumps against bid to eradicate red state's Democrat-held districts
Politics.detail: A Republican speaker responded to Booker's opposition to a plan aimed at eliminating Democrat-held districts in a red state.

Politics.headline: Virginia Supreme Court strikes down Democrats' redrawn US House maps, giving Republicans a win
Politics.detail: The Virginia Supreme Court has overturned the Democrat's redrawn US House maps, resulting in a victory for Republicans.

Politics.headline: Italy-US ties strained as pope and Iran war dominate talks
Politics.detail: Discussions between Italy and the US are focused on the Pope and the ongoing war with Iran, creating tension between the two nations.

Finance & Economy.headline: US economy added 115,000 jobs in April, beating expectations
Finance & Economy.detail: Hiring once again exceeded forecasts, with employers adding far more than the projected gains of 65,000, signaling continued economic strength.

Finance & Economy.headline: U.S. International Trade Deficit Increased in March 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: The U.S. monthly international trade deficit increased in March 2026, rising from $57.8 billion in February to $60.3 billion, as imports increased more than exports.

Finance & Economy.headline: Worried Britons 'prepping' for major disruption with stash of tins and cash, survey shows
Finance & Economy.detail: A recent survey indicates that many Britons are stocking up on essential supplies like canned goods and cash in anticipation of potential major disruptions.

Finance & Economy.headline: Nike faces class action lawsuit accusing it of pocketing tariff refunds while charging consumers more
Finance & Economy.detail: Nike is facing a class action lawsuit with accusations of retaining tariff refunds while continuing to increase prices for consumers.

Finance & Economy.headline: GDP (Advance Estimate), 1st Quarter 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: Real gross domestic product (GDP) increased at an annual rate of 2.0 percent in the first quarter of 2026, according to the advance estimate released by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis.

Technology.headline: Instructure hackers claim they stole data from nearly 9,000 schools
Technology.detail: Hackers locked students out of Canvas and warned the company to negotiate with them by May 12, claiming they stole data from nearly 9,000 schools.

Technology.headline: The Morning After: Google's new wearable doesn't have a screen
Technology.detail: This week's biggest stories include Google's new wearable, the new reMarkable slate, and a new gimbal from DJI.

Technology.headline: Twitch has new penalties for streamers caught viewbotting, CEO says
Technology.detail: Repeat offenders will be punished with "caps" on their views.

Technology.headline: OpenAI debuts a Codex plugin for Chrome
Technology.detail: The new feature could turn Codex into more than a coding aid.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Tests Windows Feature That Could Make App Startup Less of a Slog
Technology.detail: Microsoft is testing a new Windows feature designed to improve app startup times.

Science.headline: Physicists May Have Just Cracked Open a Hidden Side of the Quantum World
Science.detail: For decades, every known particle was thought to belong to one of two categories -- bosons or fermions -- but researchers have now shown that bizarre “in-between” particles may exist.

Science.headline: Listening to workout music can dramatically boost endurance.
Science.detail: A new study shows that listening to your own favorite workout music can dramatically boost endurance, with cyclists exercising with self-selected songs lasting nearly 20% longer than when riding in silence, yet they didn't feel more exhausted.

Science.headline: Smarter, Safer Way to Wipe Out Termites
Science.detail: Scientists may have found a smarter, safer way to wipe out termites hiding inside homes using a chemical called bistrifluron which prevents drywood termites from forming new exoskeletons during molting, killing entire colonies from within.

Science.headline: Fur seals bodies are secretly working overtime after exhausting hunting trips
Science.detail: Scientists discovered that hours after returning to land, the fur seals' bodies are secretly working overtime.

Science.headline: Ancient Humans Were Making Surprisingly Advanced Stone Tools During Ice Age
Science.detail: Scientists in China discovered that ancient humans were making surprisingly advanced stone tools during a harsh ice age 146,000 years ago, created by Homo juluensis, show careful planning.

Health.headline: 'Waiting a long time': Advocates happy to see HIV declared public health emergency in Manitoba
Health.detail: The Manitoba government declared a public health emergency due to rising cases of HIV in the province, with the 2024 infection rate being roughly 3.5 times higher than the national average. The province reported 328 new HIV cases in 2025, more than double the 142 cases recorded in 2021.

Health.headline: Hantavirus-hit ship expected in Canaries on Sunday: Spanish health minister
Health.detail: The cruise ship MV Hondius, linked to a hantavirus outbreak, is expected to arrive at Granadilla port in Tenerife on Sunday, with authorities monitoring the situation. Upon arrival, passengers will disembark wearing face masks, and health officials will check for symptoms of the virus.

Health.headline: Thalassemia patients in Gaza face worsening health crisis amid medicine shortages
Health.detail: Medical sources in Gaza report that shortages of specialized medication, laboratory supplies, and blood units are putting thalassemia patients' lives at serious risk, with 50 out of 334 registered patients having died since the war began. International health organizations are urged to intervene and provide essential medicines and medical supplies as Gaza’s healthcare system deteriorates.

Health.headline: Health officials discuss smoking, vaping challenges
Health.detail: While cigarette smoking has declined among young people in Chautauqua County, vaping has increased, with about 10% of teenagers self-reporting vape use. Health officials are emphasizing prevention efforts, noting that flavored nicotine vapes contain more nicotine than traditional cigarettes, and that most adult smokers began before age 18.

Health.headline: President off to UK for health check-up
Health.detail: President Mohammed Shahabuddin left Dhaka for London for follow-up medical treatment and a health check-up. He is scheduled to return home on May 18 after completing the necessary health tests at a specialized hospital in the UK.

Sports.headline: Knicks Take 3-0 Lead Over 76ers
Sports.detail: Jalen Brunson scored 33 points, helping the New York Knicks secure a 108-94 victory over the Philadelphia 76ers in Game 3 of their series, giving them a commanding 3-0 lead in the Eastern Conference semifinals. Brunson sealed the game with important plays late in the game, delighting the home crowd.

Sports.headline: Simranjeet Kaur Advances to Archery World Cup Semi-Final
Sports.detail: India's Simranjeet Kaur has progressed to the semi-final of the women's recurve individual event at the Archery World Cup Stage 2 held in Shanghai. This marks a significant achievement for Kaur on the international stage.

Sports.headline: India U17 Women Qualify for AFC Quarter-Finals
Sports.detail: The Indian Under-17 Women's team made history by qualifying for the quarter-finals of the AFC U-17 Women's Asian Cup for the first time, achieving a dominant 4-0 victory against Lebanon in their final Group B match in Suzhou, China. This is a major milestone for women's football in India.

Sports.headline: Shamrock Rovers Extend Lead in League of Ireland Premier Division
Sports.detail: Shamrock Rovers have extended their lead in the League of Ireland Premier Division following a busy night of action. St Pat's are keeping up the pressure in the league.

Sports.headline: Kalyn Ponga Reacts to Tom Dearden's Injury News
Sports.detail: Kalyn Ponga was visibly stunned after learning that Tom Dearden would miss the State of Origin series opener due to an ankle injury. The news came during a press conference following Ponga's strong performance in Newcastle's 44-10 win over the Dragons.

Entertainment.headline: Bonnie Tyler's Health: Updates After She Was Placed in Medically Induced Coma
Entertainment.detail: The "Total Eclipse of the Heart” singer Bonnie Tyler was reportedly put into a medically induced coma during recovery from emergency intestinal surgery.

Entertainment.headline: Canceled TV Shows of 2026: Which Series Are Ending This Year?
Entertainment.detail: 'The Witcher' is among the high-profile shows entering their final season in 2026.

Entertainment.headline: Bad Bunny at the Met Gala 2026: See Photos of His Aged Look
Entertainment.detail: See photos of Bad Bunny's decades-aged appearance at the 2026 Met Gala.

Entertainment.headline: Stephen Colbert's Final 'Late Show' Guests Revealed
Entertainment.detail: Stephen Colbert is preparing to say goodbye to 'The Late Show,' and his final guests have been announced.

Entertainment.headline: Raja Shivaji BO Day 9 [LIVE]: Film is set to cross Rs 70 cr gross
Entertainment.detail: Riteish Deshmukh's historical drama 'Raja Shivaji' is making waves at the box office, entering its second week with impressive earnings and is set to cross Rs 70 cr gross.

Crime & Justice.headline: Senior Sri Lankan monk arrested for alleged child sex crimes
Crime & Justice.detail: Pallegama Hemarathana, the chief prelate of Colombo, has been arrested for allegedly sexually abusing an 11-year-old girl in a temple, marking the highest-profile case involving a local religious leader. Authorities took the 71-year-old monk into custody from a private hospital in Colombo, where he had checked in for treatment as a criminal probe against him progressed.

Crime & Justice.headline: Two men charged in connection with filming antisemitic TikTok videos
Crime & Justice.detail: Adam Bedoui, 20, and Abdelkader Amir Bousloub, 21, are due to appear at Thames Magistrates' Court on Saturday, 9 May 2026, charged with religiously aggravated harassment offences after allegedly travelling to a predominately Jewish area to film antisemitic social media videos. The charges relate to allegations that Jewish people in Stamford Hill, north London, were approached, harassed, and filmed.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man charged after making alleged antisemitic gestures
Crime & Justice.detail: Zakos Michael is scheduled to appear at Highbury Magistrates' Court on Saturday, May 9, 2026, charged with racially aggravated harassment for allegedly making antisemitic gestures and shouting abuse in a predominantly Jewish residential area on May 7. The Crown Prosecution Service has decided to charge Zakos Michael with racially aggravated harassment in connection with the incident.

Crime & Justice.headline: How MAGA Sheriff Chad Bianco is shaking up the 2026 California gubernatorial primary
Crime & Justice.detail: Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco is running for governor of California on a tough-on-crime platform closely aligned with President Trump’s agenda. State data indicates that violent crime rose during much of his tenure as sheriff, and his department cleared fewer reported crimes than other sheriff's departments, though more recent data suggests a turnaround.

Crime & Justice.headline: Justice Department Moves to Denaturalize 12 Individuals for Concealing Terrorist Support, War Crimes, Espionage, Sexual Abuse, and More
Crime & Justice.detail: The Department of Justice announced that it filed denaturalization actions in various U.S. district courts against 12 individuals accused of serious offenses, including providing material support to a terrorist, war crimes, espionage and sexual abuse. The individuals are alleged to have obtained their status through fraud or by lying about past criminal acts, or because they demonstrated allegiance to terror groups after obtaining their citizenship.

Education.headline: New Jersey graduation rate reaches record high, state education officials say
Education.detail: New Jersey schools reported gains in graduation rates, dual enrollment participation and career readiness indicators during the 2024-2025 school year, according to data released by the New Jersey Department of Education, with the statewide graduation rate reaching an all-time high of 91.8% in 2025. State officials also reported declines in chronic absenteeism and disciplinary incidents compared with recent years.

Education.headline: Govt moves to reform education policies: State minister
Education.detail: State Minister for Primary and Mass Education Bobby Hajjaj said the government is bringing changes in education policies to curb existing problems in the sector, including teacher shortages and improving education quality. The government is considering reformulating education policies to address these existing problems.

Education.headline: TRIO McNair Scholars Program Celebrates Largest Graduating Cohort Since 2022 Relaunch
Education.detail: North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University's TRIO Ronald E. McNair Post-Baccalaureate Achievement Program is celebrating its 2026 graduating class, which is the largest cohort of graduates since the program's relaunch in 2022. Since relaunching in 2022, the program has expanded opportunities for students interested in research, graduate education, and professional development.

Education.headline: Maharashtra 10th Board Results: Around 80,000 Students Fail In Marathi Paper
Education.detail: Approximately 80,000 students in Maharashtra failed the Marathi language paper in the 10th board exams. The Maharashtra Class 10 re-evaluation window is now open.

Education.headline: Sabah Education Minister defends 'no dilapidated schools' remark after backlash
Education.detail: Sabah Education, Science, Technology and Innovation Minister Datuk James Ratib clarified his statement that there are no longer any “dilapidated schools” in Sabah, explaining that the issue involves dilapidated buildings within schools, not entire schools being unsafe. He stated that the government's focus is on replacing old wooden blocks with safer concrete structures, rather than replacing entire schools.

Lifestyle.headline: Mother's Day 2026: Date, history, significance and how the occasion is celebrated worldwide
Lifestyle.detail: Mother's Day in 2026 will be observed on Sunday, May 10, and is a day centered around appreciating mothers and maternal figures for their care, emotional support, and stability. The occasion is celebrated every year on the second Sunday of May in many countries, including India, the United States, and Australia.

Lifestyle.headline: Rock hunter says artifacts are closer than you may think
Lifestyle.detail: Brea Wear of Hanska, a rock hunter, says that you don't have to go far from home to find interesting rocks, fossils, and other artifacts, including bison teeth, as there are nice agates around the Minnesota and Cottonwood River sandbars and gravel roads near New Ulm. She created a Facebook page titled “River Valley Lapidary” that displays her wire wrap pendants and polished gemstones.

Lifestyle.headline: Over 500 breakfasts served at Sentimental Journey's event
Lifestyle.detail: The Sentimental Journey hosted its pancake breakfast on Sunday, May 3, inside the hangar at the William T. Piper Memorial Airport and sold more than 500 breakfast tickets, possibly the biggest all-you-can-eat breakfast Sentimental Journey has sponsored in roughly a decade. With a large team of volunteers, the non-profit was able to provide a quality, all-you-can-eat breakfast for over 500 people, all for a good cause.

Lifestyle.headline: Chinese actress Esther Yu is eager to take on new challenges and diverse roles
Lifestyle.detail: Chinese actress Esther Yu expressed appreciation for the warm reception she received from fans in Malaysia and did not rule out the possibility of filming there. She conveyed her happiness in meeting her Malaysian fans and expressed hopes to return soon.

Lifestyle.headline: L.A. Affairs: We were integrating our worlds and families. Then came the boob texts
Lifestyle.detail: A woman recounts her experience of integrating her life with a man she met running, until she discovered explicit texts and photos to and from 13 other women on his iPad. She left him, but is grateful for the lessons learned about the importance of integrity and asking questions upfront in relationships.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: Fixing how Colorado funds nonprofits will strengthen communities
Opinion/Editorial.detail: House Bill 1274 aims to address the challenges nonprofits face with the reimbursement-based structure of state grants by creating more consistency and preserving flexibility in funding, ensuring that public dollars reach communities when they are needed most.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Resisting the rat invasion
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Increased rat sightings in Taipei have sparked concerns over urban sanitation and the risk of hantavirus, prompting calls for a measured and multifaceted approach encompassing coordinated sanitation, stricter enforcement of waste management regulations, and greater emphasis on each person's role in maintaining community hygiene.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Endorsement: There's only one candidate capable of filling Pelosi's shoes in Congress. That's Scott Wiener
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The San Francisco Chronicle's editorial board has endorsed Scott Wiener for Congress, highlighting his effectiveness as a legislator focusing on key issues like universal healthcare and housing, while questioning other candidates' readiness for the role.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Zombies are running the international system
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The post-war international system is unraveling under renewed superpower rivalry, with multilateral institutions like NATO, the WTO, and the UN resembling the walking dead, as key agreements and norms are undermined.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The real reason Trump doesn't want Congress to vote on war powers
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Donald Trump is hesitant to have Congress vote on war powers related to the conflict in Iran because the war is unpopular and Republican members of Congress fear the political repercussions of supporting it, especially with midterm elections approaching.

World.headline: Trump Announces Three-Day Ceasefire in Russia-Ukraine War
World.detail: President Trump announced that Russia and Ukraine have agreed to a three-day ceasefire and a prisoner swap of 1,000 prisoners from each side. The ceasefire aims to allow for prisoner exchanges during the Victory Day weekend.

World.headline: Spain's Canary Islands Brace for Incoming Hantavirus-Stricken Cruise Ship
World.detail: The Canary Islands are preparing for the arrival of a cruise ship affected by a hantavirus outbreak, with health officials taking precautions to contain the spread. Several passengers have been evacuated and are receiving medical care as authorities work to manage the situation.

World.headline: Indonesia's Mount Dukono Erupts, Killing 3 Hikers
World.detail: Mount Dukono in Indonesia erupted, resulting in the death of three hikers and injuries to others. Rescue operations are underway to assist those affected by the volcanic eruption.

World.headline: U.S. Awaits Iran's Response on Peace Deal as Month-Long Ceasefire Holds
World.detail: The United States is currently awaiting a response from Iran regarding a proposed peace deal, as a month-long ceasefire remains in effect. However, tensions remain as the U.S. military says its forces fired on two Iran-flagged oil tankers to stop them from docking at an Iranian port.

World.headline: Russia Holds Downsized Victory Day Parade
World.detail: Russia held a scaled-down Victory Day parade in Moscow, commemorating the Soviet Union's victory over Nazi Germany in World War II. The parade occurred amidst a temporary ceasefire with Ukraine.

Zimbabwe.headline: From free beer to the altar: Harare has all the plans this weekend
Zimbabwe.detail: Harare offers a range of weekend activities, from enjoying free beer to attending weddings.

Zimbabwe.headline: Sweet success: Kathy Mwanza adds innovation to Zim's baking industry
Zimbabwe.detail: Kathy Mwanza is bringing innovative ideas to Zimbabwe's baking industry.

Zimbabwe.headline: Buses, bonuses and bragging rights: Harare derby ignites
Zimbabwe.detail: The Harare derby is generating excitement with buses, bonuses, and bragging rights at stake.

Zimbabwe.headline: 25 years with HIV: Samanga's story of survival and strength
Zimbabwe.detail: Samanga shares her story of survival and strength after living with HIV for 25 years.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe Returns Farms Seized From European Owners To Push for Debt Relief
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe will return 67 foreign-owned farms and pledge $146 million in compensation, primarily targeting properties under treaties with Denmark, Switzerland, Germany, and the Netherlands, in an effort to mend relations with the West and unlock international lending to alleviate its $13 billion foreign debt.
